The Proposal at a Glance

What
AI Agent Team

Deploy a team of AI agents on dedicated Westfield hardware. Agents work inside Trello and Teams — monitoring boards, generating research, building presentations, processing expenses, and identifying vendors.

Human review on everything that goes out.

Cost
~$206/month
  • •Mac Mini M4 — ~$900 one-time
  • •LLM API — hard-capped at $200/mo
  • •1 M365 account — $6/mo

No open-ended spend. Increases require separate approval.

Why Now
Competitive Gap

Competitors are deploying agent teams today — compounding a velocity advantage every month we don't. We've already proven the approach works on real Westfield data over the past 3 weeks:

  • ✓ Shelf scanning & pricing
  • ✓ Patent FTO research
  • ✓ Expense processing
  • ✓ Vendor identification
  • ✓ 98-board operations dashboard

8-week phased rollout. Go/no-go at every phase. Human reviews everything until trust is earned.

3 / 19

AI Assistants vs. AI Agents

🧑 Conventional AI — ChatGPT, Claude
You work for the AI.
  • ✕Open a browser, ask a question
  • ✕Wait for answer
  • ✕Copy/paste results into your systems
  • ✕Repeat for every task
  • ✕You are the integration layer
Limitations
  • •No permanent context with your company
  • •No memory between sessions
  • •Can't act autonomously
  • •Requires manual workflow every time
🤖 Deployed AI Agents — This Proposal
AI works for you.
  • ✓Agents run on Westfield hardware 24/7
  • ✓Access Trello, email, SharePoint, databases
  • ✓Work autonomously on assigned tasks
  • ✓Remember context and projects
  • ✓Integrated into your workflows
Capabilities
  • •Permanent context on Westfield hardware
  • •Auto-generate decks and research reports
  • •Coordinate multi-step projects
  • •Monitor boards, email, and market trends 24/7

The difference: ChatGPT is a tool you use. AI Agents are team members who use tools.

6 / 19

How It Works — Two Interaction Models

Model 1
Trello-Native

Most workflows — no new tools required

1 Card lands on agent board (via webhook)
2 Agent picks it up — reads card context & attachments
3 Does the work — research, analysis, or content creation
4 Posts results to card — comments, attachments, report
5 Moves card to Human Review — you approve & ship

Live example

Patent FTO — copy card → agent runs USPTO search → FTO report with citations → reviewer comments → agent revises within minutes.

Model 2
Teams Conversational

Direct chat — agent responds like a colleague

1 Team member messages agent in Teams
2 Agent evaluates complexity — simple or multi-step?
3 Spawns worker agent if complex task — parallelizes work
4 Worker completes task — returns results to orchestrator
5 Results delivered back in Teams — within minutes

Live example

"Riley, what are competitors doing in rocking chairs?" → research delivered in Teams chat within minutes, no context switching.

Trello is the workflow engine. Teams is the conversational layer. Both feed the same agents.

7 / 19

Architecture

Mayor — Orchestration Layer

Routes work behind the scenes, tracks multi-agent projects, and handles quality review. Users never interact with Mayor directly.

Trello — The Database

Standard pipeline per workflow: Memory → Input → Processing → Human Review → Done

Webhooks trigger agents on card moves — near-instant response.

Agents automatically select the right model tier based on task complexity.

Agent Model Stack — Right model, right task
Tier Role Example Use
OrchestrationMayor, routing, QACoordinating multi-agent patent analysis
Deep ResearchLong context, reasoningUSPTO FTO with 200k token patent corpus
WorkhorsePresentations, reportsSell sheets, vendor emails, listings — 80% of tasks
VisionImage understandingShelf scan — 15 products ID'd, 14/15 priced
Image GenProduct visualsConcept renders, deck graphics, mood boards
FastCron, routing, checksMatt's daily board digest — zero LLM cost

Agents auto-select tier based on task complexity. No manual model selection required.

8 / 19

Model Cost Optimization

Right-sizing model usage keeps costs predictable and controlled.

Fast Model

Cron jobs, notifications, status checks

60%
of tasks · cheapest
Workhorse Model

Presentations, reports, translations

30%
of tasks · mid-range
Premium Models

Patents, deep research, orchestration

10%
of tasks · most capable
Predictable Monthly Spend — ~$206/mo breakdown
~$6
Fast model tasks
60% of volume, pennies/run
~$80
Workhorse model
30% of volume, most spend
~$20
Premium + M365 license
10% of tasks, high value

Usage-based API pricing. No seats, no per-user licenses, no surprise overages. Scales with workload, not headcount.

Hard cap: $200/month on API spend. 90% of work runs on cost-efficient models. Any additional spend requires separate approval and will be similarly capped — no open-ended bills, ever.

9 / 19

What We've Already Proven

01 — Shelf Scanning
Video → Branded Report

Upload a store walkthrough → frames extracted → AI vision IDs products, brands, prices → branded Snapshot Report.

15 products identified at Target · 14/15 matched with real API prices · 100% private label mapping at Academy

02 — Patent Research
Card → FTO Report

Copy a card → agent runs USPTO search → FTO report with citations posted as comments → reviewer feedback loop closes in minutes.

Full FTO report in <15 minutes vs. weeks manually · Tested on rocking chair portfolio (GCI/Grace patents)

03 — Innovation Collaboration
Comments → Edits → Changelog

Team comments on a Trello card → agent edits presentations → replies with a full changelog of every change made.

Live with design team on Innovation S28 · Handles multi-round revision loops autonomously

04 — Expense Processing
Accurate to the Penny

Receipts → company Excel template with correct mileage rates, per diem, and categorization. All tests accurate to the penny. Enforces expense policy automatically — e.g. strips alcohol from meal receipts before submission.

100% accuracy in testing · Auto-enforces expense rules · Correct $0.725/mi rate · Routes approval → email

05 — Operations Dashboard
98 Boards · Daily · Zero LLM Cost

All 98 org boards scanned daily. Health scores, stale card detection, activity heatmap, charts, narrative exec briefing.

33 API calls for entire org · Batch-optimized · $0 LLM cost per daily run · Runs on cron

06 — Vendor Identification
Source → Qualify → Sample

Identifies potential vendors via web research, initiates contact through Outlook, qualifies capabilities, and requests samples — managing 8+ conversations across 6 countries autonomously.

8+ vendors identified & contacted · 6 countries · Tracks state per vendor · Human reviews before send

Trello Board Architecture

Standard pattern for every agent workflow — consistent and predictable.

Standard Pipeline — Applied to Every Agent Board
📋 Memory
→
📥 Input
→
⚙️ Agent Processing
→
👁 Human Review
→
✅ Done
5 Active Agent Boards — Standard 5-List Structure
🔬 Patent Research

Trigger: card moved to In Analysis

Memory · In Analysis · Human Review · Done · Archive

📸 Shelf Scan

Trigger: video attachment detected on card

Memory · Input Hopper · Agent Processing · Human Review · Done

💳 Expense Reports

Trigger: receipt uploaded to card

Report Creation · Agent Processing · Needs Info · Human Review · Done

💡 Innovation S28

Trigger: team comments on presentation card

Memory · Drafts · In Review · Human Review · Done

📦 Product Hub

Trigger: weekday cron + source card changes

Memory · Active Products · Archive

+ More boards as workflows approved

Webhook-driven — card moves trigger agents in seconds. Human always reviews before anything goes out.

14 / 19

Security & Compliance

Three enforcement layers — security at infrastructure level, not prompt level.

Layer 1
M365 Permissions

✓ Allowed

  • · Product specs & marketing content
  • · Designated agent workspaces (full)
  • · Shared product development boards

✕ Blocked

  • · Customer contracts & pricing
  • · Financials, HR, legal documents
  • · Executive communications
Layer 2
Trello Board Segmentation

✓ Agent boards (member)

  • · Patent Research, Shelf Scan
  • · Expense Reports, Innovation S28
  • · Agent test boards only

✕ Excluded (enforced at board level)

  • · Costco Program, Walmart Retail
  • · Any customer-confidential board
  • · Executive & finance boards
Layer 3
LLM Provider — Anthropic Enterprise

✓ Guaranteed

  • · SOC 2 Type II certified
  • · Zero data retention policy
  • · Enterprise DPA in place

✕ Never happens

  • · No training on our data
  • · No storing inputs/outputs
  • · No sharing with 3rd parties

Security enforced at infrastructure level, not prompt level. No relying on the AI to "behave."

15 / 19

Investment

One-Time Hardware — ~$900
Mac Mini M4
  • ·Apple M4 chip — 16-core Neural Engine
  • ·24GB unified memory — runs all agents simultaneously
  • ·512GB SSD — agent files, logs, outputs
  • ·Dedicated always-on host — no tying up a work machine
  • ·Low power, silent, fits in a desk corner
Monthly Recurring — ~$206/mo
LLM API (Claude) ~$106/mo
M365 agent account ~$22/mo
Trello + misc APIs ~$78/mo

Usage-based. Scales with work volume, not headcount.

The cost of waiting is real — and it's growing.

Shelf scan: 15 min vs. half a day manually. Amazon's team runs this every week.

Patent FTO: overnight vs. a week of attorney time. Competitors know their IP exposure. Do we?

Trend research: 2 days for competitors vs. 2+ weeks for us. That's seasonal windows closing.

16 / 19

Deployment Timeline

8-week phased rollout — each phase is a go/no-go checkpoint.

Phase 1 · Weeks 1–2
Infrastructure
  • ·Mac Mini M4 setup & on-network
  • ·M365 agent account provisioned via C2IT
  • ·Teams bot registered & permissioned
  • ·Production boards created with webhook triggers

Milestone: Infrastructure ready, first webhook fires

Phase 2 · Weeks 3–4
First Agents on Production Boards
  • ·Priority agents live on real production data
  • ·Riley, Matt, Patent Research go first
  • ·Live demo with Darin + leadership
  • ·Feedback loop tightens agent quality

Milestone: Go/no-go checkpoint — live demo to stakeholders

Phase 3 · Weeks 5–6
Expand to Additional Workflows
  • ·Pete, Danny, Lingo, Alex onboard
  • ·Account boards added as approved
  • ·Model tuning based on real usage patterns
  • ·Teams bot conversational flows tested

Milestone: Full agent roster operating on real workflows

Phase 4 · Weeks 7–8
Full System Operational
  • ·All approved agents in production 24/7
  • ·Monitoring, alerting, and logging in place
  • ·Team trained on Trello + Teams interaction
  • ·Graduated trust review cycle begins (Month 1–2)

Milestone: System live — agents working as teammates

Each phase is a go/no-go checkpoint. Proof of concept validates the approach — production adapts tested capabilities to real volumes.

17 / 19

Graduated Trust

Trust is earned through demonstrated performance — not assumed.

Month 1–2
100% review
Build Confidence

Every piece of agent output goes through Human Review before use. Team sees quality firsthand.

  • ·Auto-delivers: Nothing yet — everything reviewed
  • ·Held for review: All output — reports, emails, decks
  • ·Success metric: >90% output accepted without major edits
Month 3–4
~35% auto-deliver
Selective Review

Low-risk routine work auto-delivers. High-stakes decisions still held for approval.

  • ·Auto-delivers: Internal reports, translations, dashboard digests
  • ·Still reviewed: Customer-facing content, pricing, IP matters
  • ·Success metric: Team spends <30 min/day on agent review
Month 5+
Exceptions only
Focus on Exceptions & Strategy

Team trusts routine output. Human judgment reserved for where it matters most.

  • ·Auto-delivers: Most reports, listings, translations, digests
  • ·Reviewed: Edge cases, IP, customer-contract-adjacent work
  • ·Success metric: Agents completing 80%+ of routine work autonomously
18 / 19

The Ask

Three things to get from proof of concept to production.

1
Mac Mini M4 — ~$900 one-time

Dedicated always-on agent host. M4 chip, 24GB RAM, 512GB SSD. Runs all agents simultaneously without tying up a work machine.

  • ✓Low power — runs 24/7 for ~$5/mo electricity
  • ✓On-network — data never leaves Westfield infrastructure
  • ✓Available for next-day delivery — zero lead time
2
1 M365 Account + Teams Bot Registration

Coordinate with C2IT to provision the agent's M365 account and register the Teams bot. Scoped permissions — IT controls access from day one.

  • ✓Standard M365 E3 license (~$22/mo) — no special tier
  • ✓Bot registration takes <1 hr with C2IT — standard process
  • ✓IT manages permissions — agent can't access restricted data
3
8-Week Deployment Sprint — Approval to Begin

Four-phase rollout with go/no-go at each checkpoint. Max leads development, Darin approves workflows, Bryan/Jordan provide air cover.

  • ✓No dedicated headcount — Max builds in parallel with current role
  • ✓Live demo at Week 4 — leadership sees agents in action
  • ✓Infrastructure already proven — not starting from zero
